purpose: Surgical Phase 10A overlay for implementation-readiness audits where current runtime inspection proves an earlier Phase 9 status was stale, or where a known wrong-shape implementation must remain blocked until corrected.
rules: These overrides are audit/status overlays only; they do not create parallel feat runtime systems.
Use existing runtime engines and registries as the source of proof.
implemented_correct overrides require concrete runtime consumers, not metadata alone.
implemented_incorrect overrides preserve fail-closed guards for known wrong-shape automation.

overrides.sourcebook: Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide
overrides.name: Implant Training
overrides.reason: Current runtime has ImplantRules, DefenseCalculator, and ActorEngine condition-shift integration. ImplantRules.hasImplantTraining suppresses active implant Will penalties and extra downward condition-track steps, DefenseCalculator includes ImplantRules.getWillDefensePenalty in Will Defense math, and ActorEngine.applyConditionShift consults ImplantRules.getConditionTrackExtraStep.
overrides.observedImplementation: Runtime suppression is implemented in existing implant/defense/condition engines; not merely metadata.
overrides.accuracyFinding: Promote from implemented_partial to implemented_correct for current repo state.

overrides.name: Advantageous Attack
overrides.reason: Keep blocked: source rule is a damage rider against an enemy who has not yet acted, while current catalog observation still identifies wrong-shape ATTACK_OPTION/attack-option metadata. Do not promote until runtime damage timing context is implemented and wrong-shape metadata is quarantined or replaced.
overrides.observedImplementation: Wrong-shape implementation remains intentionally classified as implemented_incorrect.
overrides.accuracyFinding: Phase 10A locks the guardrail so future audits do not accidentally count this as partial/correct merely because metadata exists.
